title: astelia pachysperma subsp. fallax
intro: Astelia pachysperma subsp. fallax, commonly known as the 'Mountain Astelia', is a subspecies of flowering plant that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Astelia pachysperma subsp. fallax include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
